title: Metal Gear — Online Manual: Codec
notes: Confirms the paused transceiver flow, manual tuning, memory contacts and incoming CALL sign.

title: Metal Gear 2: Solid Snake — Online Manual: Codec
notes: Official frequency list for Campbell, Miller, Kasler, Holly White and Johan Jacobsen; notes that frequencies can change.

title: Metal Gear Solid — Online Manual: Codec/Save
notes: Official support/save frequencies and Codec behavior for Shadow Moses.

title: Metal Gear Solid 2 — Online Manual: Codec/Save
notes: Official Tanker and Plant support/save frequencies plus white/red CALL behavior.

title: Metal Gear Solid 3 — Online Manual: Codec/Save
notes: Official Major, Para-Medic and The Boss frequencies and memory/call behavior.

title: Metal Gear MSX2 in-game transceiver data
notes: Used for original Outer Heaven resistance contact identities and channel values. Frequencies may change during progression.

title: Metal Gear Solid in-game Codec contacts
notes: Used for field/secret contacts not enumerated in the short manual frequency table.

title: Metal Gear Solid 2 in-game Codec contacts
notes: Used for chapter-specific field and secret contacts beyond the manual's permanent support lines.

title: Metal Gear Solid 3 in-game radio contacts
notes: Used for SIGINT and EVA chapter availability.

title: Metal Gear Solid 4 in-game communication network
notes: MGS4 uses contact selection and network/video calls rather than a classic manually dialed canonical frequency chart.

title: Metal Gear Solid: Peace Walker briefing and support files
notes: Peace Walker support is represented as MSF briefing/intel channels, not classic numeric Codec frequencies.

title: Metal Gear Solid V radio and cassette support
notes: MGSV support is represented as iDroid/radio/cassette channels. Numeric values in the simulator are routing IDs, not canon frequencies.

title: Shadow Codec Ops simulation design layer
notes: Original lore-compatible dialogue and synthetic routing identifiers created for the private simulator.
